summary: Any authenticated user can access the user-listing endpoints that appear administrative. This exposes all registered usernames, display names, user IDs, and account creation timestamps without any administrator role check.

evidence_snippets.reason: Both endpoints require only authentication and do not enforce any administrator or privileged-role authorization check.
The repository returns a full account listing, so the missing role check in the controller directly exposes all user records to any logged-in account.

root_cause: Administrative user enumeration functionality is guarded only by a generic login check and not by a dedicated authorization rule for privileged users.
repair_plan: Restrict these endpoints to explicitly authorized administrators. Add a server-side role check before calling list_users, return 403 for non-admin users, and keep any non-admin directory functionality limited to the minimum fields and scope actually required.
